channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Singapore approves caning for school bullies
item.description: The move is part of Singapore's new anti-bullying measures introduced by the Ministry of Education.

media:group.media:description: New standardised anti-bullying measures have been rolled out across Singapore schools.

item.title: Labor axes funding for $45b Inland Rail project linking Melbourne to Brisbane
item.description: The federal government has halted plans for the Melbourne to Brisbane Inland Rail, amid concerns the project could cost more than $45 billion to complete.
item.dc:creator: Lexie Jeuniewic

media:group.media:description: The Inland Rail project will no longer be funded to connect Melbourne to Brisbane.

item.title: Human-to-human transmission suspected in deadly hantavirus outbreak
item.description: The World Health Organization says no rats were found on board the cruise vessel where the outbreak has taken hold, with three people having died of confirmed or suspected infections of the rat-borne virus.

media:group.media:description: Two cases of hantavirus have been confirmed from passengers who spent time aboard the MV Hondius cruise ship.

item.title: Indigenous commissioner condemns comedian's 'deeply offensive' video
item.description: South Australia's Commissioner for Aboriginal Children and Young People speaks out against a "deeply offensive" social media video posted by comedian Alex "Shooter" Williamson in the wake of the death of Kumanjayi Little Baby.

media:group.media:description: Dale Agius has condemned the comedian's social media post. File photo.

item.title: Teaching positions, courses to go under TasTAFE cuts
item.description: TAFE teaching jobs and courses will go and consideration given to asset sales under a Tasmanian government plan to find savings.
item.dc:creator: Loretta Lohberger

media:group.media:description: Among the job losses are 19 teaching positions, four casual teaching positions, two permanent prison training officers and a fixed-term prison training officer.

item.title: Inquest into cold case hears man who hid teenager's body also lied to police
item.description: Rebecca Richardson was a beloved teenager who went missing from Gin Gin in 1998. Her body has never been found and her family is desperate for answers.
item.dc:creator: Jasmine Hines

media:group.media:description: Rebecca Richardson, 15, was last seen at Gin Gin in November 1998.

item.title: '24/7 wanding searches' of patients in hospital's behavioural assessment unit
item.description: Patients entering Canberra Hospital's Behavioural Assessment Unit will now undergo metal detector screening after a knife incident forced staff into a three-hour lockdown.
item.dc:creator: Lois Maskiell

media:group.media:description: All patients to Canberra Hospital's Behavioural Assessment Unit are now being searched with metal detection wands.

item.title: Childcare worker granted bail over alleged 'brutal assault' of children
item.description: Jayendra Shahi was charged with allegedly assaulting two girls at a childcare centre in Sydney's south-west.
item.dc:creator: Sean Tarek Goodwin

media:group.media:description: Jayendra Shahi was granted bail on Tuesday.

item.title: 'Good luck walking home': Alice Springs murder trial centres on intent
item.description: Jurors in a trial over the alleged murder of an Alice Springs teenager have heard competing arguments about how to interpret videos taken in the lead up to the young man's death.
item.dc:creator: Chris Murrkarany Fitzpatrick

media:group.media:description: Yiel Gatluak, 19, was found dead on a dirt road outside Alice Springs in 2024, with three people now facing trial charged with his murder.

item.title: 'Police can't help you': The threat issued to Melbourne's pub and club owners
item.description: Melbourne is in the grip of a crime wave. But this time it's different and pub and club owners say they are scared as 'The Cartel' issues threats they do not understand.
item.dc:creator: Alysia Thomas-Sam and Melissa Polimeni

media:group.media:description: Michael Trimble, owner of Melbourne's Bar 20, says "everyone is hoping that they're not going to be next".

item.title: Blast at fireworks factory in China kills at least 26, state media says
item.description: Authorities evacuated nearby residents and deployed rescue robots after the deadly explosion at the fireworks factory in Hunan province.

media:group.media:description: Rescuers work on site after an explosion at a fireworks plant in Liuyang.

item.title: Vietnam veteran receives Medal for Gallantry after leading bayonet charge
item.description: Vietnam veteran John O'Halloran is awarded Australia's third-highest military honour for leading a fixed bayonet charge on "one of Australia's worst days in South Vietnam".
item.dc:creator: Greig Johnston

media:group.media:description: John O'Halloran receives the Medal for Gallantry from Chris Dawson.

item.title: Concerns raised before Aboriginal teen's death, inquest hears
item.description: A 17-year-old Aboriginal boy in state care was staying in a semi-independent living facility at the time of his sudden death, despite a witness raising concerns about his lack of supervision, an inquest has heard. Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander readers are advised that this article contains the name of an Indigenous person who has died, with the permission of his family.
item.dc:creator: Eva Blandis

media:group.media:description: An inquest is being held to determine whether the death of the 17-year-old boy was preventable.

item.title: Latrell Mitchell breaks silence to talk form, positions and PNG
item.description: The NRL's strongest voice, Latrell Mitchell, breaks a lengthy league media ban to discuss his form, his positional switch and the potential of a move to PNG.

media:group.media:description: Latrell Mitchell speaks to the media for the first time in nearly two years.

item.title: Students vent frustrations over Notre Dame nursing training
item.description: Numerous nursing students at Perth's Notre Dame University say issues with work placement have left them wondering if they will be able to graduate on time.
item.dc:creator: Nadia Mitsopoulos and Emma Wynne

media:group.media:description: Nursing students have to complete 800 hours of placement in order to graduate and become registered nurses.

item.title: Taxi operator tells Victorian court wheelchair passenger deaths 'tragic'
item.description: Echuca Moama Taxi Group put wheelchair passengers at risk of death or serious injury by failing to ensure passengers were properly secured, a Victorian court has heard.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Mangelsdorf

media:group.media:description: Echuca-Moama Taxi Group will be sentenced in June.

item.title: 'Scapegoating': Staff, students and politicians back ANU interim leader
item.description: A group of politicians, unionists, students and staff come together to support the Australian National University's interim leader, expressing concerns she is being scapegoated for existing turmoil within the embattled institution.
item.dc:creator: Adam Shirley

media:group.media:description: ANU interim vice-chancellor Rebekah Brown stepped into the role last year, replacing Genevieve Bell.

item.title: Five things you can do to save money now that interest rates have gone up
item.description: Sensible Emily untangles the latest interest rate decision and gives you tips to help you save money.
item.dc:creator: Emily Stewart

item.title: Surfers Paradise ditches cars for pedestrians as esplanade closed for good
item.description: The Gold Coast council has voted to permanently close part of the Surfers Paradise Esplanade despite division over traffic and business impacts.
item.dc:creator: Dominic Cansdale and Charmayne Allison

media:group.media:description: The Esplanade in Surfers Paradise on a Saturday morning during the trial closure.

item.title: Puppy torturing couple heckled by locals after bail renewed
item.description: Nathan James Dekkers and Isabel Maree Busher will remain on bail, with sentencing for their involvement in the mistreatment and death of an 11-week-old puppy adjourned until October.
item.dc:creator: Georgia Loney

media:group.media:description: Composite image of Busher and Dekkers leaving court in Busselton

item.title: NT debt to hit $12.55b as budget delivers no new cost of living relief
item.description: The Northern Territory's debt is set to climb to $12.55 billion next financial year, as the 2026-27 budget delivers record spending on health and crime prevention without providing any new cost of living relief.
item.dc:creator: Roxanne Fitzgerald

media:group.media:description: This year's budget has delivered record spending on health and law and order.

item.title: 'She needs to go': Calls for Margot McNeill to resign from CIT
item.description: Canberra Insititute of Technology's chief executive Margot McNeill is under pressure to resign, after the ACT Legislative Assembly released a damning report which found she misled the institute's board.
item.dc:creator: Niki Burnside

media:group.media:description: CIT chief executive Margot McNeill was found to have misled the institute during her recruitment process.

item.title: Carlton fined $75,000 over Elijah Hollands incident
item.description: Three weeks on from Elijah Hollands's mental health episode at the MCG, the AFL fines Carlton $75,000 for its handling of the player and issues around the incident.

media:group.media:description: Elijah Hollands played on for Carlton against Collingwood in round six despite having a mental health episode.

item.title: Nicky Winmar's barrister claims AFL great was defending himself
item.description: The barrister for Nicky Winmar has told a Victorian court the former AFL great was defending himself on the night he allegedly assaulted a woman.
item.dc:creator: Anna McGuinness

media:group.media:description: Nicky Winmar has pleaded not guilty to charges including unlawful assault.

item.title: Philip Morris secret hearing angers health advocates over double standard
item.description: The federal health minister and health groups are raising concerns about a secret appearance of cigarette giant Philip Morris at a parliamentary inquiry examining black market tobacco.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Asher, Stephanie Borys, and Katie McAllister

media:group.media:description: Leah Blyth says transcripts of the evidence given by Philip Morris will be released.
